series_description:
Including any supporting documentation
created and/or used to justify and support
legislative appropriations requests by an
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVAL NOTE: The archive
requirement is met by UT System
sending the required copies of the
requests to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program,
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission. Only copies of
supporting documentation
submitted to the Legislative Budget
Board are archival.

series_description:
Any document that officially initiates,
rescinds, or amends general office
procedures.

Any document that initiates, rescinds, or
amends a regulation, policy, or procedure
that governs the programs, services, or
projects of an agency.

series_title:
Calendars, Appointment,
Special Event Planning and
Itinerary Records
series_description:
Calendars, appointment books or
programs and scheduling, or itinerary
records maintained by staff during
business hours that document
appointments, itineraries, and other
activities of agency officials or employees.
Special events are events that are planned
by a department, such as retirement and
recognition ceremony for staff personnel.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected
officials, agency heads, and board
or commission members require
archival review. Contact UT Tyler
Archivist when records have met
retention requirements.
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds but
used by a state official or employee
to document his or her work
activities may be a state record and
subject to this retention period.

series_description:
News, press releases, marketing files and
collateral, or any public relations files
maintained or issued by an agency.
Includes print, electronic, audio, Social
Media records, and audiovisual records.
Including media guides, press releases,
etc.

Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are furnished to the public
under the Public Information Act (Chapter
552, Government Code). Also includes
withdrawn requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are excepted under the Public
Information Act (Chapter 552,
Government Code), including records
provided to the legislature under section
552.008.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Charts or diagrams that show the
structure of an agency and the
relationships and relative ranks of its
departments and job positions.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts
showing the overall arrangement
and administrative structure of the
state agency need to be transferred
to the University Archives and
Special Collections (UASC) in the
Muntz Library.

series_description:
Plans and records relating to the process
of planning new or redefined programs,
services, or projects of an agency that are
neither included in nor directly related to
other records series in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_description:
Notes or text of speeches, papers,
presentations, or reports delivered in
conjunction with agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by
or on behalf of executive staff,
board of commission members,
division directors and program
heads require archival review.

series_title:
Litigation Files, including
Medical Liability claims and
litigation files
series_description:
Records created by or on behalf of an
agency in anticipation of or in the
adjudication of a lawsuit or records
created by or on behalf of an agency in
anticipation of or in the adjudication of a
medical malpractice/professional liability
lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years
comments:
All statutes of limitation must be
met before file is closed. Review
before disposal; some cases that
set legal precedent may merit a
review by University Archivist for
historical value. ARCHIVES NOTE:
Cases that set legal precedent or
exhibit historical value will be
evaluated by the University
Archivist for archival preservation.

series_description:
Logs or similar records used to register
persons appearing before state agencies
as required by Chapter 2004, Government
Code, including quarterly reports filed
with the Texas Ethics Commission (TEC).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Operational strategic plans, which include
descriptions of information resources
programs, prepared in accordance with
Government Code, 2054.095 and
2056.002. Includes working files and
related documentation used in creating
the final plan.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
The archival requirements are met
by sending the required copies of
the plans to the Texas State
Publications depository program.
The archival requirement is met
both by sending copies to the
Publications Depository and by
sending a copy to the University
Archives.

series_description:
Self-evaluations and plans documenting
compliance with the requirements of the
Americans with Disabilities Act.

series_title:
Meetings, Agendas and
Minutes of Open Meetings
series_description:
Agendas and minutes of state boards,
committees, commissions, and councils
that conduct open meetings as required
by Chapter 551, Government Code.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Agency retains
permanent record copy. The
archival requirement will be met by
sending a copy to the University
Archives and Special Collections
(UASC).

series_description:
Minutes of internal agency staff meetings
and supporting documentation during
which formal minutes are taken.

Biennial narrative reports to the governor
and legislature as required by an agency's
enabling statutes, including annual
narrative reports if they are required by
statute. Includes working files and related
documentation used in creating final
report.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of these agency reports must be
submitted to the UASC and the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3. The
requirement is met by sending the
required copies of the reports to
the Texas State Publications
Depository Program, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.

series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules,
or similar records distributed internally for
the use of employees or externally to the
public or those individuals or entities
regulated by an agency that sets out the
rules, policies, and procedures that govern
an agency's programs, services, or
projects. Includes working files and related
documentation used in creating rules,
policies, and procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final rules, policies,
and procedures, are not subject to
archival requirement and may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description:
Reports made to the Office of Attorney
General (OAG) on an agency's Public
Information Act activities, per
Government Code, 552.010.

Transcripts and final decisions of hearings
conducted as part of the regulatory
process, and hearings on proposed rules
and changes.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only records that
lead to substantial or statutory
changes to the operations of UT
Tyler and its policies and
procedures should be retained for
University Archivist review. Contact
the University Archivist when these
records have met their retention
periods. CAUTION: These records
may be maintained with related
information, including meeting
notices, proofs of publication, and
meeting minutes, and require a
longer retention period.

series_title:
Sunset Review Report and
Related Documentation
series_description:
Sunset Review Report, agency self-study,
and other correspondence and supporting
documentation related to the Sunset
review process for state agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Related
documentation includes the agency
self-evaluation report, agency
response to the commission's draft
report, and documentation of the
agency's implementation of the
commission's final
recommendations.

series_description:
Final agreement described by Government
Code, 2009.054(c), associated with a
matter conducted under an alternative
dispute resolution procedure in which
personnel of a state agency participated
as a party on the agency's behalf.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
legal_citation:
Texas Civil
Practice and
Remedies Code,
154.071.

series_description:
Patent, trademark and copyright records
which pertain to employees’ original work.
May include but not limited to a copy of
the work itself as submitted to the United
States Copyright Office and the
corresponding copyright application,
registration notice, and supplementary
documents.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Master versions of all forms used
internally and externally by the agency,
including subsequent revisions to an
agency form or any associated design or
design modification requests or any
periodic listing of all forms used internally
or externally by an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
A records retention schedule (i.e., form
SLR 105) that identifies the records that
are created and maintained by an agency,
provides the minimum timeframes the
records must be retained, and includes
instructions for their disposition. This
series may include working files and
documentation of certification and
approval by the Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.
comments:
Original is retained for 50 years by
the State and Local Records
Management Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.

series_description:
Forms used to track the transmittal of
records to/from onsite and offsite storage
areas or a transfer of physical custody.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Logs or similar records listing records
disposed of by an agency, which might
include records series title, dates of
records, and date of disposition.
comments:
One copy should be with
originating department, one copy
should be with Records Manager.
CAUTION: Disposition can mean
destroyed or transferred.

series_description:
Worksheets or working papers used to
capture records inventory information,
including location tracking and to
document existence and requirements of
a records series.

Documentation used to provide and
improve access to records. Includes
indexes, card files, shelf lists, registers,
guides, and other finding aids.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: These records must
carry the same retention period
and archival code of the records
they support.

series_description:
Records management plans and similar
records that establish the policies and
procedures under which records and
information are managed in an agency.

Transmittals and related service
documentation (e.g., form RMD109) for
disaster recovery services provided by
TSLAC or other entities.

Forms used by TSLAC (e.g., form RMD
113) or other entities to establish disaster
recovery services, authorize agency staff
to access the media, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Relatively long-lived computer files
containing organized and consistent sets
of complete and accurate electronic
records and associated processing files.
Examples include, but are not limited to,
data tables of relational databases used by
applications or computer programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers must be certain before
assigning the retention period of
AC to agency master files and
application data that the files do
not fall under records series listed
elsewhere. Original artwork,
including photo prints and
negatives that have significant
value as evidence of agency
programs as well as the potential
for re- use, may be archival.

series_description:
Agency-developed automated software
applications, operating system files, and
associated processing files, including job
control language, programs, applications,
scripts, source code, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records documenting operational and
maintenance requirements of computer
hardware such as operating manuals,
hardware/operating system requirements,
hardware configurations, equipment
control systems, and associated
processing files.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records adequate to specify all technical
characteristics necessary for reading or
processing electronic records; to
document modifications to computer
programs; to access, retrieve, manipulate,
and interpret data in an automated
system; and allow for their timely,
authorized disposition. Records include
but are not limited to: user guides, system
or sub-system definitions, system
specifications, input and output
specifications, system flow charts,
program flow charts, program
maintenance logs, change notices, data
element dictionaries, file layouts, code
books or tables, and other records that
explain the meaning, purpose, structure,
logical relationships, and origin of the data
elements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware and software
needed for access to electronic
records must be retained for the
period of time required to access
the records.

series_description:
Files needed for electronic data audits.
Records include but are not limited to:
reports showing transactions accepted,
rejected, suspended, and/or processed;
history files or tapes; records of updates
to application files; and security logs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
Some information may be exempt
from public disclosure, for
example: information that controls
security access to selected areas.

series_title:
Chargeback Records to Data
Processing Services Users
series_description:
Records documenting usage, costs, billing,
cost recovery, budgeting, and
administrative functions of computer
usage and data processing services for
individual units / departments / divisions
in an agency.

series_title:
Data Input Documents/Batch
Data Entry Control Records
series_description:
Forms and logs used to reconcile batches
submitted for processing against batches
received and processed. May include but
not limited to: Daily charge batch log, daily
charge documents delivered to PFS, daily
verify, Output Records for Computer
Production (Reports showing transactions
that were accepted, rejected, suspended,
and/or processed), etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_title:
Software Registrations,
Warranties, and License
Agreements
series_description:
Records documenting the registration and
licensing of a software application to
activate the software for legal use by the
end users of a state agency, along with
warranties providing that the software will
perform in accordance with functional
specifications.

Records documenting the request for and
response to help desk tickets received by
divisions or units, such as information
technology.

Biennial information security plan for
protecting the security of the agency’s
information.

Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents
whose submission by candidates for
vacant positions is required on the
application form, by application
procedures, or in the employment
advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Includes Temporary Employment
and Certificate of Age (minor
workers)

series_description:
Notes, memoranda, or reports relating to
the counseling of an employee for work-
related, personal, or substance abuse
problems. Usually maintained at the
supervisorial level except in those
agencies with counseling staff.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
Period is AC+7 if counseling
provided by, and records kept by a
licensed psychologist as required by
22 Tx Admin Code 465.22 (d)(2).

series_description:
Internal or external announcements or
advertisements of job openings,
promotions, training programs, or
opportunities for overtime.

Includes a contract or agreement
regarding the terms and
conditions of employment of an
individual and a contract with a
vendor for temporary staffing
services.
Executed, renewed, or amended
on or after September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Includes a contract or agreement
regarding the terms and
conditions of employment of an
individual and a contract with a
vendor for temporary staffing
services.
Executed, renewed, or amended
on or before August 31, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:
NOTE: Refer to SB20 (84th Leg.) for
retention period context.

series_description:
Job evaluations, performance appraisals,
or other similar documents used to
evaluate the performance of an employee
or faculty.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Disciplinary actions are those actions
which may affect pay, status, or tenure.
They are imposed to discipline an
employee whose conduct is harmful to the
best interests of the state, the agency, or
the employee work force; and for failure
to improve performance or conduct
following imposition of corrective action.
May include cause for demotion,
suspension, or dismissal, and the reasons
for failure to give written notice of
resignation. May also be used to
document evidence of employee self-
improvement efforts, as well as favorable
and unfavorable communication.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Forms or similar records used to create or
change information concerning the
records of an employee, including pay
grade, position classification, employee
number, evaluation date, and termination
of employment.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments: PAF or eForm.

series_description:
Job descriptions, including all associated
task or skill statements, for positions in an
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Criminal history record information on job
applicants or agency employees obtained
from the Department of Public Safety
(DPS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is
authorized to obtain a criminal
history record information from
DPA must refer to its agency's
legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code for
appropriate retention and use of
this information.

series_description:
Certificates of completion, certifications,
licenses, transcripts, test scores, or similar
records documenting the training, testing,
certification, licensing, or continuing
education achievements of an employee.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Documentation or Verification
of Employment Eligibility
series_description: Federal reporting form (Form I-9).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Federal regulation
requires that INS I-9 forms be
retained for 3 years from date of
hire or 1 year after separation of
the employee, whichever later.
Agencies should make certain that
the INSF I-9 forms for employees
who terminate from an agency less
than 3 years from date of hire are
kept for the 3 year retention
period.

series_description:
Background information packets. Originals
are sent to University of Texas System -
Office of the Director of Police or UTS-
ODOP.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Summary of apprenticeship applicant
qualifications, evaluation basis for
selection or rejection, original
applications, job assignments, promotions,
separations, layoffs, terminations,
compensation records, conditions of work,
time records, hours of training provided
(separate from labor), minority status, and
gender of all selected and rejected
applicants.
Awards, incentives, tenure, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Employee Drug Testing and
Screening Records – Positive
and Calibration
series_description:
Records of employee alcohol test results
indicating an alcohol concentration of 0.02
or greater; records of employee verified
positive controlled substances test results;
documentation of refusals to take
required alcohol and/or controlled
substances tests; employee evaluation
and referrals; calibration documentation;
records related to the administration of
the alcohol and controlled substances
testing programs; copy of each annual
calendar year summary.
legal_citation:
49 CFR 382.403
for commercial
motor vehicle
drivers.

series_title:
Employee Drug Testing and
Screening Records – Negative
Results
series_description:
Records of negative and canceled
controlled substances test results and
alcohol test results with a concentration of
less than 0.02. All negative results of a
drug test fall under the Drug Free
Workplace Act or as required for
Commercial Driver's License or other
drivers under United States Department
of Transportation regulations as well as
records related to cancel tests.

Agreements between employee and
agency authorizing certain actions,
including external employment/conflict of
interest acknowledgement forms,
telecommuting agreements,
outside/secondary employment
authorizations, or other documentation
that show proof of receipt and awareness
of and adherence to agency policies and
procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Employee or applicant requests for
reasonable accommodation under the
ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
This series documents the activities and
administration of an Institution's
department or office's volunteer program.
Records may include: volunteer hour
statistics; volunteer program publicity
records; insurance requirements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Documentation used to start, modify, or
stop all voluntary or required deductions
from payroll, including garnishment or
other court-ordered attachments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Payroll records and registers documenting
employee earnings, wages, and pay. This
may include but is not limited to payroll
input records, summary statements,
payroll vouchers, payroll detail sheets, and
payroll history.
comments:
All audit requirements must be
met.

series_description:
Includes but not limited to Forms W-2,
1042-S, 1099-Misc., 1099-INT, 1098-T,
FICA and supporting working papers
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Used to make increases or decreases to
employees' gross pay, FICA, retirement, or
in the computation of taxes.

Employer's copy of "Employee’s
Withholding Exemption Certificate."
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Records defining the wage or salary rate
for each position in the agency expressed
in dollars, grades, or step numbers.

Records and documentation relating to
unemployment compensation claims,
including reimbursement of funds
disbursed by Texas Workforce
Commission (TWC).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Records documenting the amount of
pension or deferred compensation earned
by individual employees.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
For instructions regarding the
determination of the closure of
accounts and for additional
information regarding the retention
period see the most current edition
of the Benefits Coordinator
Reference Manual issued by the
Employees Retirement System of
Texas

series_description:
Affirmative action plans for regular
employees and apprenticeship programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments: For apprenticeship plans.

series_description:
Affirmative action plans for contractors
and subcontractors.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Reports providing statistical information
on labor force.

Records relating to review and monitoring
of job classifications within an agency.

Work, duty, shift, crew, or case schedules,
rosters, or assignments.

Reports to the agency from TWC or its
predecessor pertaining to employees.

Requests and authorizations for travel;
participation in educational programs,
workshops, or college classes; or for other
work-related activities for which the
expenses of the employee are defrayed or
reimbursed.
comments:
Includes request for tuition
assistance.

series_description:
Any internally distributed manuals,
guidelines, or similar records that define
agency wide policies and procedures
concerning the personnel of an agency.

Any documents detailing the procedural
duties and responsibilities of agency
positions on a position-by-position basis.

Any reports compiled by an agency on
aspects of personnel staffing, including
listings of all staff by program or name,
staff hired during a month, detailed
listings of employees within its
organizational structure, position
vacancies, analyses of turnover rates and
seasonality of employment, etc.

Aptitude, competency, or skills tests and
checklists required of job applicants or of
current personnel to qualify for promotion
or transfer, including validation records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: One copy of each
different master test (different in
terms of either questions or
administration procedures) should
be retained for the period
indicated.

series_title:
Aptitude and Skills Tests (Test
Papers & Validation Records)
series_description:
Completed aptitude, competency, or skills
test papers and checklists of job applicants
or of current personnel taking a test to
qualify for promotion or transfer.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_title:
Equal Employment
Opportunity (EEO) Reports and
Supporting Documentation
series_description:
Includes documentation used to complete
EEO (Equal Employment Opportunity)
reports.

Reports, studies, aggregated or summary
data, and similar documentation compiled
to monitor and demonstrate compliance
with the federal Equal Pay Act.

Diversity and recruitment plans and
related workforce analyses.

series_title:
Labor Condition Applications
and Public Inspection Files (H-
1BS AND E-3S) (Federal
Department of Labor)
series_description: Public Labor files of non-citizens.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Used to create employee leave balances,
to transfer leave balances when an
employee transfers positions, to adjust
carry-over balances at end of fiscal year,
to correct errors on leave accumulation,
and to close out leave accounts on
separated employees.

Cumulative report is issued each pay cycle
and provides employee leave status
information for each position.

Records documenting individual
employee’s hours worked, including work
schedules and documentation evidencing
adherence to or deviation from normal
hours for those employees working on
fixed schedules. Including time cards, time
cards, and time records.

series_title:
Time Off and/or Sick Leave
Requests
series_description:
Requests and authorizations for vacation,
compensatory, sick, Family and Medical
Leave Act (FMLA), sick pool leave, and
other types of authorized leave, and
supporting documentation.

Records documenting number of hours
transferred in and out of comprehensive
sick leave pool.

Claims; invoices; statements; copies of
checks and purchase orders; expenditure
authorizations; ledgers; encumbrance,
purchase, general journal, expenditure,
and special vouchers; freight bills paid;
and similar records that serve to
document disbursements, including those
documenting claims for and
reimbursement to employees for travel
and other expenses.
comments:
All audit requirements must be
met.

series_description:
Includes bill copies or stubs, statements,
billing registers, account cards, deposit
warrants, cash receipts, credit card
receipts, receipts books, cash transfers,
daily cash reports, reconciliations, general
journal vouchers, purchase vouchers,
special vouchers, and similar records that
serve to document money owed to or
received by a state agency and its
collection or receipt.
comments:
All audit requirements must be
met.

series_description:
Annual Financial Reports (AFR) required
by the General Appropriations Act (100
Day Report). These reports are publicly
posted on UT Tyler Financial Services
webpage.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Annual Financial Reports must
be submitted to the University
Archives per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(a).
Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final plan are not subject to archival
review and may be disposed of at
the expiration of the retention
period.

series_description:
Periodic reports compiled from

revoke their waivers of rights of
access may not see letters of
recommendation during the time
the waivers were in force.

series_description:
Athletic history of each athlete who has
competed at the institution. Frequently,
this series is a continuation of the
recruitment file and includes recruitment
records if an athlete signs a letter of
intent. May include but not limited to:
academic major information, including
performance reports; admissions
verification reports; academic transcripts;
financial aid information; recruitment
information documents; medical and
injury reports; media articles;
photographs; release of information
forms; Athletic participation reports;
EADA [EQUITY IN ATHLETICS ANALYSIS]
DOCUMENTS; personal data
questionnaires; and records of awards.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only public profile
records are subject to archival
review. Confidential information
should be removed or redacted
upon archival transfer.

series_description:
Strategies and game plays for each game
in the season.

Recruitment of athletes into the
institution's intercollegiate athletics
program. The series also provides a record
of the recruitment process for prospective
players created by the institution to
comply with NCAA, NAIA and conference
rules and regulations. May include but not
limited to: the institution's football
questionnaire forms with personal,
scholastic, football, general, and transcript
release information; information request
cover sheets; grade transcripts;
Information for Certification of NCAA
Freshman Athletics Eligibility Compliance;
letters of intent; copies of admissions
forms and materials; and ongoing
performance and eligibility reports.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Records of elections held by various
faculty and staff organizations. May
include but not limited to: ballots;
tabulations; and related documentation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
